rationale: Both reviews are grounded and actionable with concrete file/line suggestions, but Y engages more directly with the GT's own concerns (critiques the half-used CuMemTag enum, flags that the new hardware-gated tests never actually exercise the pure-Python guard logic in CI). On the latent gap, Y's top finding — that sleep() defaults to level=2, so the new strict NotImplementedError guard bricks the default sleep/wake cycle for ordinary callers — is a near-exact match to the real post-merge incident (#4905/#4912), making it a much stronger and more central gap_hit than X's speculative, hedged note about an unverified HTTP wakeup endpoint (which X's own grep found no evidence of). X's findings (stale docs example, dead `_is_sleeping` assignment, docstring drift) are valid but lower-impact and more tangential to what reviewers actually flagged.
